title: Fix the docker-related logrotation config
bodyText:
From Ansible README:
For those used to /usr/bin/chmod remember that modes are actually octal numbers.
You must either add a leading zero so that Ansible's YAML parser knows it is an octal number (like 0644 or 01777), or quote it (like '644' or '1777') so Ansible receives a string and can do its own conversion from string into number.
The error was in using 644 mode instead of 0644

title: CI performance impovements
bodyText:
The following has been done in order to improve CI performance:

Introduced an Initialize job that initializes the project with dependencies and compiles the contracts

The result, saved in CI cache, is used by subsequent build, lint,  test and cover jobs


Yarn's cache is saved in CI cache to use in every Initialize run
Compiled contracts are saved in CI cache to use in every Initialize run

The cache is based on a key with commit hash of the contracts, so if we update contracts submodule then the cache will be invalidated


Saving yarn install:deploy output in a CI artifact in order to get rid of large wall of text in CI logs coming from node-gyp that does not conform to --silent switch

E2E

The e2e sub-projects cannot make use of the Initialize job because all of the initialization, installing dependencies, compiling contracts happens in the docker build system.
Enabling docker layer caching allows to cache built docker layers in the e2e jobs run

Apparently the docker layer caching performance varies, not always the layers are taken from cache. I have included slower and quicker runs in the results



Notes

From what I've read the docker layer caching feature might become a paid feature in the future
I didn't find any more improvements, also I don't think that we can share pulling parity, redis etc. docker images between the e2e jobs. I'd say we close #65 but keep eye on further opportunities for improvement

Results
Before (current master)

After, slowest run

After, quicker oracle-e2e run

After, quicker ui-e2e run

title: [WIP] [DISCUSSION] Deployment testing
bodyText:
Purpose
The purpose of this PR is to have an early preview and discussion point about the work I started doing regarding deployment. Any comments/questions about the general direction would be appreciated.
I also have some problems and I would appreciate any tips/suggestions.
@akolotov @patitonar @phahulin @ArseniiPetrovich
Related issues

#11
#57
#9

Changes
This PR is branched out of e2e-commons #86 , which changes are not listed here.

Moved deployment/oracle/* into deployment/*

The idea is to have only one main playbook site.yml
Have different host groups that would share roles and vars:

oracle
(to be added in #9) ui
(possibly) monitor




Merged deployment/README.md and deployment/oracle/README.md
#57 Introduced production ui/Dockerfile, use it in e2e instead of Dockerfile.e2e
Introduced dev deployment setup

Dev deployment setup
The idea is to have local docker containers running that would pretend to be oracle/ui/monitor host servers, and then run site.yml playbook against them. That way there is no need to spin up a VM in AWS or DigitalOcean in order to run the playbook against it.
This approach can be used to have deployment tested on CI. The first step in this direction I have in mind is to:

Run the playbooks against local docker containers
Assert that there were no errors in the playbook

What's been done so far

Introduced dev.yml playbook

It spins up local docker container pretending to be oracle server


Introduced dev-hosts.yml inventory - containing our local oracle server
Successfuly run the site.yml against dev-hosts.yml inventory

I had to add some setup steps in dev.yml to make it work, like installing rsyslog in the oracle server - it probably needs to be moved to site.yml
I also had to modify the site.yml playbook a little bit to make it work here - like docker service run step



Problems
When trying out this approach, I run into very severe performance issues related to (I think) Docker-In-Docker situation.
We have a docker container that is an oracle-host, and then the site.yml playbook in role jumbox builds and run docker in it, which is a Docker-In-Docker.
The building process takes ridiculously long (both in CI on linux and on my macOS).
A simple Dockerfile step like COPY yarn.lock . can take tens of seconds...
I tried increasing memory and CPU cores dedicated to docker on my machine - monitoring with docker stats it looks like it was not runnning out of memory.
It's worth noting that we already have a Docker-in-Docker situation - if we take a look at oracle-e2e job in CI, we have a circleci/node:10.15 docker container, and in this container we run yarn run oracle-e2e - which leads to building and running docker containers. And it's not slow like that.
So I guess that there must be something wrong. The slowness can be seen here on CI.
The jumpbox : Build the container task takes > 15 minutes, whereas locally (after docker system prune -a for fair comparision) it takes me 6 minutes to cd oracle && docker-compose build.

title: Improve sender retry strategy
bodyText:
See this comment. When a tx fails, on rabbitmq a new queue is dynamically created (if not existed) with a message ttl calculated based on the retries attempts of that transaction. Once the message reaches the ttl, it is redirected to deadLetterExchange which will send the message back to the original queue that the sender is consuming. This new queue dynamically created will be autoremoved after a time of no activity.
Closes #94

title: Bump handlebars from 4.1.2 to 4.5.3
bodyText:
Bumps handlebars from 4.1.2 to 4.5.3.

Changelog
Sourced from handlebars's changelog.

v4.5.3 - November 18th, 2019
Bugfixes:

fix: add "no-prototype-builtins" eslint-rule and fix all occurences - f7f05d7
fix: add more properties required to be enumerable - 1988878

Chores / Build:

fix: use !== 0 instead of != 0 - c02b05f
add chai and dirty-chai and sinon, for cleaner test-assertions and spies,
deprecate old assertion-methods  - 93e284e, 886ba86, 0817dad, 93516a0

Security:

The properties __proto__, __defineGetter__, __defineSetter__ and __lookupGetter__
have been added to the list of "properties that must be enumerable".
If a property by that name is found and not enumerable on its parent,
it will silently evaluate to undefined. This is done in both the compiled template and the "lookup"-helper.
This will prevent new Remote-Code-Execution exploits that have been
published recently.

Compatibility notes:

Due to the security-fixes. The semantics of the templates using
__proto__, __defineGetter__, __defineSetter__ and __lookupGetter__ in the respect that those expression now return
undefined rather than their actual value from the proto.
The semantics have not changed in cases where the properties are  enumerable, as in:

{
  __proto__: 'some string'
}

The change may be breaking in that respect, but we still only
increase the patch-version, because the incompatible use-cases
are not intended, undocumented and far less important than fixing
Remote-Code-Execution exploits on existing systems.

Commits
v4.5.2 - November 13th, 2019
Bugfixes

fix: use String(field) in lookup when checking for "constructor" - d541378
test: add fluent API for testing Handlebars - c2ac79c

Compatibility notes:

no incompatibility are to be expected

 ... (truncated)

title: Oracle upgrade script to migrate from 1.1.1 to 1.2.0-rc0
bodyText:
This script is intended to upgrade an existing xDai bridge oracles from the release 1.1.1 deployed through the standard deployment procedure to 1.2.0-rc0. It uses the tokenbridge oracle docker image instead of cloning the git hub repo.
The script performs the following actions:

update the poabridge service (assumes it is in the default location: /etc/init.d/poabridge) to use a new docker compose environment
generate the new new docker compose environment to introduce new workers
pull the new TokenBridge oracle docker image
link new workers with the new docker image

The instruction to use this script was provided on the TokenBridge forum.
In the future similar script could be provided to simplify the migration process to next releases.

title: Fix some of the possibles failure reasons in e2e tests
bodyText:
Seems that the usage of 3 validators in e2e tests (merged in #264) introduced several bugs to the testing workflow.
First, there are pieces of code in the tests, that are waiting for several blocks to be mined. It can happen so, that a couple of blocks won't be enough for validators to submit all required signatures. And since oracles are configured to wait 1 block confirmation, this leads to a deadlock.
Second, isValidatorDuty in BridgeValidators.sol can be called by several validators in different blocks. In case of all validators are unlucky, nobody will call swapTokens and the corresponding test will fail.
The following changes should fixed above problems:

Moved blocks generation script in docker. Used this script in oracle-e2e, monitor-e2e
Mocked BridgeValidators.sol for oracle-e2e

title: Add Burner Wallet Plugin for WETC Bridge
bodyText:
Relates to omni/tokenbridge-contracts#365
This work was originally submitted on a separate repo and now integrated into the monorepo. Also, the burner-wallet dependencies were updated to the latest version.
The plugin adds the following definitions to be used by the existing Exchange plugin:
Assets:

ETC
ERC677
WETC

Gateway:

EtcGateway

Pair:

Bridge
WETCBridge

Project structure

tokenbridge-plugin plugin definition
local-wallet Burner wallet using the plugin with a Native - ERC20 bridge deployed in Sokol - Kovan on top of AMB.
basic-wallet Burner wallet using the plugin with the existing WETC Bridge in mainnet and Classic.

I tested the plugin in Sokol - Kovan and also with the WETC Bridge in mainnet and Classic and it worked correctly.
Here are some notes from my initial testing related more to the Burner Wallet user experience rather than the plugin itself:

When instead of using the PK, Metamask or Nifty Wallet is used, the popup to sign the transaction is not triggered when using different networks than the required for the exchange transaction and no feedback is provided. I guess this behavior is Ok but giving some feedback to the user that it is in an incorrect network would help. This is related to the official exchange plugin. After upgrading the exchange version, now a message is displayed when the wallet is not on the correct network.
When using Nifty wallet, the popup to sign the transaction is not triggered for the exchange transaction even when it is set to the correct network. It works correctly when using Metamask. I think it is probably related to the official metamask-plugin. This was fixed with the latest version of Nifty wallet and now works correctly.
For all cases, after the exchange transaction is performed and mined there is no feedback to the user. You have just to wait until the balances are updated. In this case of bridges, you can see one balance reduced and after a couple of seconds, the other balance is updated with the bridged tokens. This is related to the official exchange plugin, I guess a possible improvement to propose would be to allow Exchange Pairs to set a feedback message after the execution so the user can be informed on the delay due to the bridge operations.

title: Add support for STAKE token ERC677-to-ERC677 through AMB mode in Bridge UI
bodyText:
Closes #318
Closes #316
This PR provides:

Correct functionality to bridge STAKE tokens on both directions
Correct information displayed in the main page
Correct information displayed in the statistic page
Fixed typo in the statistic page

Pending:

Information in the Events and Status page will be addressed in a different PR

Question:

Should we create an issue to update ui-e2e tests to include tests for this mode? Should we also add an ultimate e2e test for this mode?

There is a deployed UI with tests contracts in Sokol-Kovan: https://stake-demo-5bffb.web.app/
This is how the statistics page looks:

Tokens Withdrawals, Tokens Deposits, Total Bridged, and Users are calculated by listening to TokensBridged event in home and foreign mediator contracts.
Withdrawal Fees are calculated by listening to BridgeTokenRewardAdded event in the home block reward contract.
Home and Foreign tokens amount are calculated by calling totalSupply() of each token which is requested to also display it on the main page.
Looking at the blockscout and etherscan APIs there are no methods to get data related to events generated by custom contracts that can help us to avoid querying that information directly to the blockchain. These APIs will also be re-evaluated when working on the Events and Status page.
If we want to avoid querying and perform the calculation to generate the statistics in the UI, one option could be to have a custom backend service that can provide this data directly. It should keep track of these events and provide an API so the UI can request the calculated values to show them without any other calculation. In this direction, we can investigate The graph protocol or creating our own custom solution.

title: Add ALM failed validator transactions detection
bodyText:
Built on top of #356
This PR adds the following features:

Detects failed transactions from validator when trying to verify the messages
Detects failed transactions from validator when trying to execute signatures in the foreign network

For the failed transactions for verification in home network it supports the blockscout API. It allows us to filter the transactions by timestamp. So it filters from the moment of the transaction to the next day to reduce the amount of data requested. We can review this parameter and/or approach looking for a better one.
https://github.com/poanetwork/tokenbridge/blob/6185969b09350aaa0aa90e191cb61eece428491a/alm/src/utils/getConfirmationsForTx.ts#L64-L65
For the failed transactions for executing signatures in the foreign network, it supports the Blockscout API and the Etherscan API. Here it also filters using timestamps. In this case, it looks for the next 3 days after the CollectedSignatures event timestamp. We can review this parameter and/or approach looking for a better one.
https://github.com/poanetwork/tokenbridge/blob/3cb4a655a07b58bf6d4f0bafc7af690d3a0d0c30/alm/src/utils/getFinalizationEvent.ts#L56-L57
In the case of using Etherscan, it only allows to filter by blocks and not by timestamp. So in that case, it first calls the Get Block Number by Timestamp endpoint to get the block numbers.
The changes are included in the demo: https://alm-demo-e0998.web.app/
Detecting pending transactions will be added in a separate PR.

bodyText:
This PR migrates the CI configuration from CircleCI to GitHub Actions. Closes #411

An example of the successful run: https://github.com/k1rill-fedoseev/tokenbridge-ci/actions/runs/234414305
The following secrets are required for this configuration: DOCKER_USERNAME and DOCKER_PASSWORD. The CI runner will use docker hub for caching required e2e images.

As a part of the CI migration, the following optimisations were made:

Metamask extension used in ui-e2e tests, was repackaged to the crx3 format, that is only supported in the newest browsers. Now ui-e2e tests can be run on the latest Google Chrome version, which comes preinstalled in hosted runners.
Docker images base was bumped from node:8 to the node:10.
Built and caching process was drastically improved by building only the images those parts were changed.
Ultimate tests were improved by utilising the already built oracle image instead of building a fresh one.

Default file mode in ansible copy command was changed from 0666 to 0600. So, I have explicitly specified the 0666 mode in these cases as it was suggested by the corresponding warning.
monitor-e2e tests for erc-to-native mode were fixed by adding a missing if statement.

bodyText:
This PR allows validator to create a special files, allowance_list.txt and block_list.txt, which will filter out some of the CollectedSignatures requests.

If allowance_list.txt is not empty, then only requests with either sender or recipient being in the list will be processed.
If block_list.txt is not empty, then only requests with neither sender nor recipient being in this list will be processed.
In all other cases, every request is processed as it is.

Handle JSONRPC error codes in web3 providers
bodyText:
The Infura provider reports error codes as an response for a JSON RPC request. E.g.
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2020 09:48:24 GMT
< Content-Type: application/json
< Content-Length: 88
< Connection: keep-alive
< Vary: Origin
< 
* Connection #0 to host mainnet.infura.io left intact
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32603,"message":"request failed or timed out"}}

This PR is to let the oracles to to rely on the returned error code to switch to a fallback RPC URL.

bodyText:
This PR is to address behavior when due to congestion in the chain, the privileged oracles' transactions with 0 gasprice could getting stuck. Later, when the oracle is trying to resend them it will get error message that the transaction with the same hash is already in the txpool, and it will not be added back to the resend queue for further check if it was eventually mined.
As the result another transaction of the same oracle with the same nonce will re-write the original transaction and the bridge message will not be transferred at all.

I have found a couple of problems related to the usage of useEffect ReactJS hooks in the ALM app. This PR should address them, the number of made requests decreased in my local tests.
I also believe that previously used subscribe/unsubscribe pattern does work properly. If unsubscribe() is called while the Promise in useEffect is still in the pending state, once finished, it will create a new setTimeout subscription, while it should not do so. title: Add blocks range limit for logs polling
bodyText:
Addresses the issue found under #555
Polling events from the unbounded block range can be a source of several problems:

Some RPC endpoints have a strict limit for the maximum range limit for eth_getLogs (e.g. standard BSC JSON-RPCs)
If requested block range contained lots of events, oracle won't be able to handle all of them in the fixed amount of time (defined by ORACLE_MAX_PROCESSING_TIME). It will fail to make any progress from such point.

This PR fixes both problems by introducing a configurable range size limit for both sides.

Changelog

fix weird error in integration tests - eb860c0

(POSSIBLY) BREAKING CHANGES:

the changes from version 4.6.0 now also apply
in when using the compile-option "strict: true". Access to prototype properties is forbidden completely by default, specific properties or methods
can be allowed via runtime-options. See #1633 for details. If you are using Handlebars as documented, you should not be accessing prototype properties
from your template anyway, so the changes should not be a problem for you. Only the use of undocumented features can break your build.

This PR adds a web3 provider wrapper that intercepts and modifies eth_getLogs requests into a batch of two simultaneous requests (eth_getLogs + eth_blockNumber). This is useful for recognising unsyncronized node state, when eth_getLogs toBlock parameter is less than latest available block on the particular node.

title: Allow to store validator keystore file in the docker swarm secret
fix/bsc rate limit
Refetch old logs ranges to see if there are missed events
bodyText:
The idea was to introduce a way of refetching old block ranges, without generating a lot of useless RPC requests. Here is how it can be achieved:

Oracle records all seen events in the Redis sorted set, where set score for each event is its block number, and the value is its unique identifier (e.g. txHash + logIndex)
Once per Y blocks, oracle re-requests logs for (HEAD - X - Y)..(HEAD - X) period instead of following regular logs processing iteration. For each event, it checks if its identifier is present in the Redis sorted set. Then it reprocesses all events which were not present in Redis.
Oracle removes checked range of events from the Redis sorted set, so that its size will not grow indefinitely.

This way, usual circumstances will require only a single RPC request per Y blocks to be made, which is very efficient.

title: Missing case when check nonce error
bodyText:

title: Bump json5 from 0.5.1 to 1.0.2
bodyText:
Bumps json5 from 0.5.1 to 1.0.2.

Release notes
Sourced from json5's releases.

v1.0.2

Fix: Properties with the name __proto__ are added to objects and arrays. (#199) This also fixes a prototype pollution vulnerability reported by Jonathan Gregson! (#295). This has been backported to v1. (#298)

v1.0.1
This release includes a bug fix and minor change.


Fix: parse throws on unclosed objects and arrays.


New: package.json5 has been removed until an easier way to keep it in sync
with package.json is found.


v1.0.0
This release includes major internal changes and public API enhancements.


Major JSON5 officially supports Node.js v4 and later. Support for Node.js
v0.10 and v0.12 have been dropped.


New: Unicode property names and Unicode escapes in property names are
supported. (#1)


New: stringify outputs trailing commas in objects and arrays when a space
option is provided. (#66)


New: JSON5 allows line and paragraph separator characters (U+2028 and U+2029)
in strings in order to be compatible with JSON. However, ES5 does not allow
these characters in strings, so JSON5 gives a warning when they are parsed and
escapes them when they are stringified. (#70)


New: stringify accepts an options object as its second argument. The
supported options are replacer, space, and a new quote option that
specifies the quote character used in strings. (#71)


New: The CLI supports STDIN and STDOUT and adds --out-file, --space, and
--validate options. See json5 --help for more information. (#72, #84,
and #108)


New: In addition to the white space characters space \t, \v, \f, \n,
\r, and \xA0, the additional white space characters \u2028, \u2029,
and all other characters in the Space Separator Unicode category are allowed.


New: In addition to the character escapes \', \", \\, \b, \f, \n,
\r, and \t, the additional character escapes \v and \0, hexadecimal
escapes like \x0F, and unnecessary escapes like \a are allowed in string
values and string property names.


New: stringify outputs strings with single quotes by default but
intelligently uses double quotes if there are more single quotes than double
quotes inside the string. (i.e. stringify('Stay here.') outputs
'Stay here.' while stringify('Let\'s go.') outputs "Let's go.")




... (truncated)


Changelog
Sourced from json5's changelog.

Unreleased [code, diff]
v2.2.3 [code, diff]

Fix: json5@2.2.3 is now the 'latest' release according to npm instead of
v1.0.2. (#299)

v2.2.2 [code, diff]

Fix: Properties with the name __proto__ are added to objects and arrays.
(#199) This also fixes a prototype pollution vulnerability reported by
Jonathan Gregson! (#295).

v2.2.1 [code, diff]

Fix: Removed dependence on minimist to patch CVE-2021-44906. (#266)

v2.2.0 [code, diff]

New: Accurate and documented TypeScript declarations are now included. There
is no need to install @types/json5. (#236, #244)

v2.1.3 [code, diff]

Fix: An out of memory bug when parsing numbers has been fixed. (#228,
#229)
